---
title: "Boolean relaxation"
subtitle: "From discrete subsets to the cardinality polytope"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
*Draft outline — content to come.*
:::

## Reparameterising the support

Replace the discrete indicator $z \in \{0,1\}^p$ by a continuous $t \in [0,1]^p$ and write

$$
\beta_j = t_j \, \xi_j,
\qquad
t_j \in [0, 1].
$$

- $t_j = 1$ means feature $j$ is **in**.
- $t_j = 0$ means **out**.
- Vertices of the hypercube ↔ discrete supports.

The cardinality constraint $\sum z_j \le k$ becomes $\sum t_j \le k$ — a polytope.

## The relaxed objective

$$
f_{\delta, \lambda}(\boldsymbol t)
=
\min_{\beta_0,\,\boldsymbol\xi}
\Bigl\{
-\tfrac{1}{n}\,\ell(\beta_0, T_{\boldsymbol t}\boldsymbol\xi)
+ \lambda\,\|\boldsymbol\xi\|_2^2
+ \delta\,\|\Gamma_{\boldsymbol t}\,\boldsymbol\xi\|_2^2
\Bigr\}.
$$

- Inner problem in $\boldsymbol\xi$: **ridge GLM**, solved by `glmnet` in one call.
- Outer problem in $\boldsymbol t$: smooth, defined on the cardinality polytope.
- $\delta$ is a curvature parameter (homotopy schedule lives here).

## Envelope gradient

By Danskin's theorem, the gradient w.r.t. $\boldsymbol t$ has closed form:

$$
g_j(\boldsymbol t) \;=\; -\,\frac{2(\lambda + \delta)}{t_j^{\,3}}\,\hat\xi_j(\boldsymbol t)^2.
$$

This is the only thing the outer Frank-Wolfe step needs.

> Placeholder for visual: the four-panel loss-landscape figure (discrete → smooth → smooth+$\ell_1$ → strongly penalised).

---

[← COMBSS preview](../motivation/04-combss.qmd) · [Next → Frank-Wolfe](02-frank-wolfe.qmd)
